verse.arabic_text: إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍۢ سَلِيمٍۢ
verse.translation: Except for one who comes to Allah with a sound heart.

theme_question.correct: The ultimate currency on the Day of Judgment is a pure and sound heart.
options: Salvation is achieved through accumulating vast wealth for charity.
Having many righteous children guarantees one's success in the afterlife.
The ultimate currency on the Day of Judgment is a pure and sound heart.
Intercession from the prophets is the only way to attain paradise.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse is part of a powerful supplication by Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham). It directly follows ayah 88, which states, 'The Day when neither wealth nor sons will be of any benefit.' Together, these verses clarify that on the Day of Judgment, all worldly status and possessions become worthless.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme centers on the 'Qalb Saleem' (قَلْب سَلِيم), the 'sound heart.' This is the ultimate currency for the Hereafter. It signifies a heart that is pure, free from associating partners with Allah (shirk), and cleansed of spiritual ills like envy, pride, and hypocrisy. The verse teaches that our eternal success depends not on our material or social standing, but on the spiritual condition in which we meet our Lord.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Ash-Shu'ara ('The Poets') is a Meccan surah focused on divine revelation and the consistent pattern of its rejection by arrogant people. It consoles the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) by detailing the struggles of earlier prophets, including Moses, Abraham, Noah, and others. The surah repeatedly emphasizes Allah's power and justice, showing how He saved the believers and destroyed the deniers, reinforcing the message that truth will ultimately prevail.
